title: lachemilla angustata
intro: Lachemilla angustata, also known as rockrose or sticky rockrose, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lachemilla angustata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in topical applications for skin issues and wound care.
